// Initial peer set: 3 pods\n        let peer_set_3pods = crate::peer_discovery::PeerSet::new(vec![\n            \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n            \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n            \"pod-3\".to_string(),\n        ]);\n        coordinator_1\n            .set_peer_set_for_test(peer_set_3pods.clone())\n            .await;\n        coordinator_2\n            .set_peer_set_for_test(peer_set_3pods.clone())\n            .await;\n        coordinator_3\n            .set_peer_set_for_test(peer_set_3pods.clone())\n            .await;\n\n        // Track which shards pod-3 owns initially\n        let mut pod3_owned_initial = Vec::new();\n        for shard_id in 0..64u32 {\n            let shard_str = shard_id.to_string();\n            if coordinator_3.owns_shard(&shard_str).await.unwrap() {\n                pod3_owned_initial.push(shard_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Pod-3 dies: remove it from the peer set\n        let peer_set_2pods =\n            crate::peer_discovery::PeerSet::new(vec![\"pod-1\".to_string(), \"pod-2\".to_string()]);\n        coordinator_1\n            .set_peer_set_for_test(peer_set_2pods.clone())\n            .await;\n        coordinator_2\n            .set_peer_set_for_test(peer_set_2pods.clone())\n            .await;\n\n        // Verify that all shards previously owned by pod-3 are now owned by pod-1 or pod-2\n        for shard_id in &pod3_owned_initial {\n            let shard_str = shard_id.to_string();\n\n            let pod1_owns = coordinator_1.owns_shard(&shard_str).await.unwrap();\n            let pod2_owns = coordinator_2.owns_shard(&shard_str).await.unwrap();\n\n            // Each previously pod-3-owned shard should now be owned by exactly one of pod-1 or pod-2\n            let owner_count = [pod1_owns, pod2_owns].iter().filter(|&&x| x).count();\n\n            assert_eq!(\n                owner_count, 1,\n                \"Shard {} (previously owned by pod-3) should be owned by exactly one of pod-1 or pod-2, but {} pods claim ownership\",\n                shard_id, owner_count\n            );\n        }\n\n        // All 64 shards should still be owned\n        let mut total_owned = 0;\n        for shard_id in 0..64u32 {\n            let shard_str = shard_id.to_string();\n            if coordinator_1.owns_shard(&shard_str).await.unwrap()\n                || coordinator_2.owns_shard(&shard_str).await.unwrap()\n            {\n                total_owned += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            total_owned, 64,\n            \"All 64 shards should still be owned after pod-3 removal, but got {}\",\n            total_owned\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// But the PR\n142\t  template's `cargo test --workspace` (`.github/pull_request_template.md:45`,\n143\t  `.github/release_pr_template.md:31`) uses default features and would **miss**\n144\t  them. So \"it executes in CI\" is true only if CI uses `--all-features`.\n145\t\n146\t### Criterion 5 — [NOT DONE — trivial]\n147\tThe stale comment at `tests/p13_8_anti_entropy.rs:241-244` still claims the test\n148\t\"uses an old API (`with_mode_a_scaling`)\" and \"requires the peer-discovery\n149\tfeature and ModeACoordinator.\" It is now misleading: the inline replacement\n150\texists at `anti_entropy.rs:1532-1798`. A 4-line edit to point there (and drop the\n151\tdead `acceptance_4` reference) closes this. Not a coverage gap.\n152\t\n153\t## Step 3 — Genuine remaining gaps (confirmed/refuted)\n154\t\n155\t### (a) No minimal-reshuffling / survivor-stability assertion — CONFIRMED\n156\t`test_mode_a_pod_reassignment` (`anti_entropy.rs:1665`) records **only**\n157\t`pod3_owned_initial` (`:1704-1711`) and re-checks **only** those shards\n158\t(`:1724-1738`). It never records pod-1's / pod-2's initial shards and asserts\n159\tthey are unchanged after the resize. A buggy implementation that reshuffled\n160\t**all** shards on every peer-set change — while preserving exactly-one-owner +\n161\tfull coverage — would still pass. The \"minimal reshuffling\" property\n162\t(`plan.md:3528`; quantified at `plan.md:1522` \"adding a 4th node moves at most\n163\t`2 × (1/4)` of shards\") is therefore un-pinned for the anti-entropy Mode A\n164\tbackground-work path. (The router's data-path rendezvous is a separate function\n165\tin `scatter/router.rs`; this gap is specifically about `mode_a_coordinator`'s\n166\tbackground-work ownership.)\n167\t\n168\t### (b) No scale-UP (3→4) case; only scale-DOWN (3→2) — CONFIRMED\n169\tOnly `test_mode_a_pod_reassignment` (3→2) exists. No test adds a 4th pod. Yet\n170\t`plan.md:1522` explicitly names the scale-**up** case as the canonical\n171\tminimal-reshuffling example, and scale-up is the more common real-world\n172\telasticity event. Untested for Mode A ownership.\n173\t\n174\t### (c) No pinned hash-vector test against `top1_by_score(hash(shard_id‖peer_id))` — CONFIRMED (lower priority)\n175\t`rendezvous_score` is private (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112`). The acceptance tests\n176\t+ `test_owns_exactly_one_peer_per_item` use the public `owns_shard`/\n177\t`owner_for_shard` API and verify **properties** (exactly-one-owner, full\n178\tcoverage, even distribution; plus determinism/peer-sensitivity/shard-sensitivity\n179\tvia `test_rendezvous_score_*` at `:317/:325/:333`) — but pin **no** specific\n180\texpected shard→owner vector. A change to the hash construction that preserves\n181\tthose properties while silently reassigning all ownership — e.g. changing the\n182\t`\"||\"` separator (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:115`) to `\"|\"`, swapping the\n183\tshard/peer write order, or reseeding `XxHash64` (`:113`) — would pass every\n184\texisting test yet break the documented `hash(s‖pid)` contract (`plan.md:3520`).\n185\tA golden-vector test (e.g. \"peers `[pod-1,pod-2,pod-3]` → shard `7` owned by\n186\t`pod-2`\") would catch this. Lower priority: guards against an unlikely\n187\thash-construction refactor, not a live regression.\n188\t\n189\t## Recommendation for child beads\n190\t\n191\tThe disabled `acceptance_4` is **redundant** — do NOT rewrite it. A buggy implementation\\nthat reshuffled **every** shard on each peer-set change — while still preserving\\nexactly-one-owner + full coverage — would pass this test.\\n\\nThe \\\"minimal reshuffling\\\" property is an explicit Mode A design invariant:\\n`mode_a_coordinator.rs:19` (\\\"ownership with minimal reshuffling\\\") and\\n`plan.md` §14.5 (cited at `mode_a_coordinator.rs:1`). Un-pinned for the\\nbackground-work ownership path. **CONFIRMED.**\\n\\n## Verdict (b) — CONFIRMED: no Mode A scale-up\\n\\nThe only Mode A peer-set *mutation* anywhere is the 3→2 downscale\\n(`anti_entropy.rs:1714`, `peer_set_2pods`). A whole-crate grep for `\\\"pod-4\\\"` /\\n`peer_set_4pods` / `4pod` returns **nothing** — no Mode A test adds a 4th pod.\\n\\nOne apparent 3→4 scale-up test exists —\\n`p4_topology_chaos.rs:282` (`chaos_rebalance_optimal_movement`, adds `node-3`) —\\nbut it does **not** cover Mode A and therefore does not refute the gap:\\n\\n- It runs in the **Rebalancer / Topology** subsystem, which never references\\n  `ModeACoordinator` / `owner_for_shard` / `rendezvous_score`\\n  (`grep` of `rebalancer.rs` / `topology.rs` for those symbols → empty).\\n- It routes through `assign_shard_in_group` → router's `score()`\\n  (`router.rs:25`, `router.rs:12`), which is a **different hash construction**\\n  from Mode A's `rendezvous_score`:\\n  - router `score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str)`: `XxHash64::with_seed(0)` →\\n    `shard_id.hash(h)` (raw `u32` bytes) → `node_id.hash(h)` (std `str` Hash,\\n    appends a `0xff` terminator). No separator.\\n  - Mode A `rendezvous_score(shard_id: &str, peer_id: &str)`\\n    (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112`): `XxHash64::with_seed(0)` →\\n    `write(shard_id.as_bytes())` (decimal string, e.g. `\\\"42\\\"`) → `write(b\\\"||\\\")`\\n    → `write(peer_id.as_bytes())`. No `0xff` terminator.\\n\\n  Different input encoding, different separator, different terminator ⇒ different\\n  scores, different code path. The p4 test validates the rebalancer's migration\\n  accounting, not Mode A rendezvous partitioning. **Mode A scale-up is genuinely\\n  untested. CONFIRMED.**\\n\\n## Verdict (c) — CONFIRMED: no pinned shard→owner golden vector\\n\\n`owner_for_shard` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:123`) is the only API that returns a\\n*concrete* owner, yet it has exactly **one** call site — internal, inside\\n`owns_shard` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:156`). No test calls it\\n(`grep -rn \\\"owner_for_shard\\\" --include=*.rs crates/ | grep -v fn` → only `:156`).\\n\\nEvery Mode A ownership test instead calls the boolean `owns_shard` and asserts\\nonly *properties*:\\n\\n- `test_owns_exactly_one_peer_per_item` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:422`): exactly\\n  one pod owns each item — never *which* pod.\\n- `test_mode_a_three_pods_each_shard_processed_once`\\n  (`anti_entropy.rs:1541`): exactly-one-owner + full coverage + even spread.\\n- `test_rendezvous_score_*` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:317/325/333`): determinism\\n  + peer-sensitivity + shard-sensitivity of the raw score — never a pinned\\n  owner.\\n- `tests/hash_fixtures.rs` only `println!`s (no `assert!`) and covers the\\n  router's key→shard *modulo* mapping, not Mode A ownership.\\n\\nSo no test pins a specific shard→owner mapping against the documented\\n`top1_by_score(hash(shard_id‖peer_id))` rule (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112-118`).\\nA change to the hash construction that preserves those properties while silently\\nreassigning all ownership — e.g. swapping the `\\\"||\\\"` separator\\n(`mode_a_coordinator.rs:115`) to `\\\"|\\\"`, swapping the shard/peer write order, or\\nreseeding `XxHash64` (`:113`) — would pass every existing test yet break the\\ndocumented `hash(s‖pid)` contract. A golden-vector test (fixed `peer_set` +\\nhardcoded expected owner per shard) is the missing guard. **CONFIRMED.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Corroboration note\\n\\nThese verdicts independently reproduce the gap analysis already recorded in the\\nsibling note `notes/bf-1zn8a.md` (step 3, lines 153-208). Nothing was refuted as\nalready-covered.\n\n---\n\n## Verdict (a) — CONFIRMED: survivor-stability / minimal-reshuffling\n\n`test_mode_a_pod_reassignment` (`crates/miroir-core/src/anti_entropy.rs:1665`)\ngoes 3→2 (kill pod-3) and asserts two things about pod-3's *old* shards:\n\n- each previously-pod-3 shard is now owned by exactly one of pod-1/pod-2 —\n  `owner_count == 1`, `anti_entropy.rs:1731-1737`\n- all 64 shards are still owned — `total_owned == 64`, `anti_entropy.rs:1751-1755`\n\nWhat it records initially is **only** `pod3_owned_initial`\n(`anti_entropy.rs:1704-1711`). It never records pod-1's or pod-2's pre-removal\nshards and never asserts they are unchanged afterward. A buggy implementation\nthat reshuffled **every** shard on each peer-set change — while still preserving\nexactly-one-owner + full coverage — would pass this test.\n\nThe \"minimal reshuffling\" property is an explicit Mode A design invariant:\n`mode_a_coordinator.rs:19` (\"ownership with minimal reshuffling\") and\n`plan.md` §14.5 (cited at `mode_a_coordinator.rs:1`). Un-pinned for the\nbackground-work ownership path. **CONFIRMED.**\n\n## Verdict (b) — CONFIRMED: no Mode A scale-up\n\nThe only Mode A peer-set *mutation* anywhere is the 3→2 downscale\n(`anti_entropy.rs:1714`, `peer_set_2pods`). A whole-crate grep for `\"pod-4\"` /\n`peer_set_4pods` / `4pod` returns **nothing** — no Mode A test adds a 4th pod.\n\nOne apparent 3→4 scale-up test exists —\n`p4_topology_chaos.rs:282` (`chaos_rebalance_optimal_movement`, adds `node-3`) —\nbut it does **not** cover Mode A and therefore does not refute the gap:\n\n- It runs in the **Rebalancer / Topology** subsystem, which never references\n  `ModeACoordinator` / `owner_for_shard` / `rendezvous_score`\n  (`grep` of `rebalancer.rs` / `topology.rs` for those symbols → empty).\n- It routes through `assign_shard_in_group` → router's `score()`\n  (`router.rs:25`, `router.rs:12`), which is a **different hash construction**\n  from Mode A's `rendezvous_score`:\n  - router `score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str)`: `XxHash64::with_seed(0)` →\n    `shard_id.hash(h)` (raw `u32` bytes) → `node_id.hash(h)` (std `str` Hash,\n    appends a `0xff` terminator). No separator.\n  - Mode A `rendezvous_score(shard_id: &str, peer_id: &str)`\n    (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112`): `XxHash64::with_seed(0)` →\n    `write(shard_id.as_bytes())` (decimal string, e.g. `\"42\"`) → `write(b\"||\")`\n    → `write(peer_id.as_bytes())`. No `0xff` terminator.\n\n  Different input encoding, different separator, different terminator ⇒ different\n  scores, different code path. The p4 test validates the rebalancer's migration\n  accounting, not Mode A rendezvous partitioning. **Mode A scale-up is genuinely\n  untested. CONFIRMED.**\n\n## Verdict (c) — CONFIRMED: no pinned shard→owner golden vector\n\n`owner_for_shard` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:123`) is the only API that returns a\n*concrete* owner, yet it has exactly **one** call site — internal, inside\n`owns_shard` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:156`). No test calls it\n(`grep -rn \"owner_for_shard\" --include=*.rs crates/ | grep -v fn` → only `:156`).\n\nEvery Mode A ownership test instead calls the boolean `owns_shard` and asserts\nonly *properties*:\n\n- `test_owns_exactly_one_peer_per_item` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:422`): exactly\n  one pod owns each item — never *which* pod.\n- `test_mode_a_three_pods_each_shard_processed_once`\n  (`anti_entropy.rs:1541`): exactly-one-owner + full coverage + even spread.\n- `test_rendezvous_score_*` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:317/325/333`): determinism\n  + peer-sensitivity + shard-sensitivity of the raw score — never a pinned\n  owner.\n- `tests/hash_fixtures.rs` only `println!`s (no `assert!`) and covers the\n  router's key→shard *modulo* mapping, not Mode A ownership.\n\nSo no test pins a specific shard→owner mapping against the documented\n`top1_by_score(hash(shard_id‖peer_id))` rule (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112-118`).\nA change to the hash construction that preserves those properties while silently\nreassigning all ownership — e.g. swapping the `\"||\"` separator\n(`mode_a_coordinator.rs:115`) to `\"|\"`, swapping the shard/peer write order, or\nreseeding `XxHash64` (`:113`) — would pass every existing test yet break the\ndocumented `hash(s‖pid)` contract. A golden-vector test (fixed `peer_set` +\nhardcoded expected owner per shard) is the missing guard. **CONFIRMED.**\n\n---\n\n## Corroboration note\n\nThese verdicts independently reproduce the gap analysis already recorded in the\nsibling note `notes/bf-1zn8a.md` (step 3, lines 153-208). This bead's added\nvalue is the explicit, cited refutation-of-the-refutation for (b): the\n`p4_topology_chaos` 3→4 test looks like it could cover scale-up, but it targets\nthe separate Rebalancer/Topology subsystem with router's `score()` — a different\nhash construction — so it does **not** close the Mode A gap.\n\n## Acceptance criteria (bf-4nybh)\n\n- [x] Verdict for (a) minimal-reshuffling: **CONFIRMED** —\n      `anti_entropy.rs:1665,1704-1711,1731-1737,1751-1755`; property at\n      `mode_a_coordinator.rs:19`.\n- [x] Verdict for (b) scale-up: **CONFIRMED** — only Mode A resize is 3→2\n      (`anti_entropy.rs:1714`); no `pod-4` crate-wide; the p4 3→4 test\n      (`p4_topology_chaos.rs:282`) uses router `score()` (`router.rs:12`) ≠\n      `rendezvous_score` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112`) and never touches\n      `ModeACoordinator`.\n- [x] Verdict for (c) pinned hash vector: **CONFIRMED** — `owner_for_shard`\n      (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:123`) never called by tests; only boolean\n      `owns_shard` asserted; rule at `:112-118`.\n- [x] Final GENUINE gaps = { (a), (b), (c) }.\n"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"755976e5-ad4b-4139-aa8f-04cd4826ebad","uuid":"d17732b2-f404-4f7a-ad79-4332c6eb6ef0"}

The one apparent 3→4 test (`p4_topology_chaos.rs:282`) does **not** refute it — it runs in the separate Rebalancer/Topology subsystem (never references `ModeACoordinator`) via router's `score()` (`router.rs:12`), which is a **different hash construction** than `rendezvous_score` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:112`): raw `u32` + `0xff` terminator vs decimal-string + `\"||\"` separator. |\n| **(c)** | pinned shard→owner golden vector | **CONFIRMED** | `owner_for_shard` (`mode_a_coordinator.rs:123`) has exactly one call site — internal (`:156`); no test calls it. Every test uses the boolean `owns_shard` and asserts only *properties* (exactly-one-owner, full coverage, determinism), never *which* pod owns *which* shard. A hash-construction change (e.g. `\"||\"`→`\"|\"`, write-order swap, reseed) would pass all tests while silently reassigning ownership. |\n\n**Genuine gaps = { (a), (b), (c) }** — none refuted as already-covered.\n\nThe verdicts independently reproduce the sibling `notes/bf-1zn8a.md` analysis; this bead's added value is the cited refutation-of-the-refutation for (b) — showing the `p4_topology_chaos` 3→4 test targets a different hash function and subsystem, so it does not close the Mode A gap.
